description: Primary-sourced visual-design rules — type scale, spacing/grid, color systems (OKLCH), hierarchy, depth. Re-derived from Refactoring UI, Modular Scale, the 8pt grid, and OKLCH primary sources.

rules.title: One modular type scale
rules.rule: Pick ONE ratio (1.125–1.333 for UI; 1.25 common) and derive ~5–7 sizes from a base; don't hand-pick arbitrary px.
rules.dont: 13/14/15/17/19/22/24 — too many near-identical sizes, no rhythm.

rules.title: Line length (measure)
rules.rule: Body measure 45–75 characters (~66 ideal); cap with max-width ~65ch. (Butterick allows up to 90ch.)

rules.title: Line height
rules.rule: Body line-height 1.4–1.7; headings tighter 1.1–1.3. Longer measures need taller line-height.

rules.title: 8pt spacing grid
rules.rule: Structural spacing is a multiple of 8px (4px fine sub-grid); pull values from a token ramp (4,8,12,16,24,32,48,64) — never arbitrary margins.

rules.title: Group with spacing (proximity)
rules.rule: Intra-group gap < inter-group gap, so related items read as a unit; a label hugs its input, groups separate.

rules.title: Hierarchy via weight/color, not size alone
rules.rule: Establish emphasis with font-weight and color/contrast, not just larger text; de-emphasize secondary text with a softer color, not a tiny size.
rules.do: Secondary text: same size, muted color (>= 4.5:1).

rules.title: Color in OKLCH tonal ramps
rules.rule: Define color tokens in oklch() (perceptually uniform), as tonal ramps (hue constant, lightness stepped evenly) + a neutral/grey ramp; 5–10 steps per hue. Avoid hand-mixed hex.

rules.title: Consistent light source + two-part shadows
rules.rule: Shadows imply a single (top) light source — offset +y; use a larger soft shadow + a tight darker one for realistic elevation. Prefer shadow/background/spacing over borders to separate.

rules.title: Design the empty + edge states
rules.rule: Every list/data view has a designed empty state (and loading + error); don't ship only the happy, full-of-data view.
